title: Bitfarms: A Bitcoin Mining Company In Transition
text: Bitfarms, one of North America's largest Bitcoin miners, announced it will gradually wind down mining operations over next two years shifting focus to high-performance computing (HPC) and artificial intelligence (AI) infrastructure. Company plans to transition from Bitcoin mining to AI infrastructure rebranding as Keel Infrastructure and planning U.S. redomiciling. BITF faces declining gross mining margins down to 35% in Q3 2025, revenue misses and continued exposure to volatile Bitcoin prices and rising energy costs. Falling Bitcoin prices and shrinking profit margins pushing operators to explore more stable revenue streams. Bitfarms secured fully funded $128 million deal with major U.S.-based data center partner to supply all necessary equipment and building materials. CEO Ben Gagnon stated conversion of just Washington site to GPU-as-a-Service could potentially produce more net operating income than company ever generated with Bitcoin mining. As of February 4, 2026, third-party Bitfarms stock predictions show wide dispersion of 12-month targets and scenario paths reflecting differing views on Bitcoin prices, mining margins and capital requirements. Other miners making similar bets with companies like Cipher and Terawulf partnering with investors like SoftBank and Google to develop AI-ready data centers attracting billions in projected revenue and unlocking additional capital through debt financing.
